text.title: AI Overview Content Template Core Rules
text.summary: Five testable rules every retrofit MUST apply. Hard rules; "stylistic preference" is not a valid reason to skip them on AIO-target pieces.

rule.statement: Every AIO-target piece MUST start with a definition block within the first 200 words of body content. The block defines the primary entity / term of the query in a single sentence (12-30 words), optionally followed by a 2-3 sentence elaboration. The block uses HTML `` / `` / `` or a clearly demarcated paragraph with the term in bold at the start.

rationale.source: AIO citation pattern study 2024-2025; Perplexity multi-source extraction

rule.rationale: AIO and Perplexity preferentially cite definition-shaped openings. The 200-word window matches the rendered preview limit. Pieces with a definition block in this window cite at multiples of those without.

rule.statement: When the query is comparison-shaped (X vs Y, alternatives to X, best X for Y) the piece MUST include a comparison table with at least 4 dimensions and at least 3 candidates. Tables use real `` markup, not formatted text. Every cell value MUST trace to a primary source citation in the citation row.
rule.rationale: AIO panels cite tables verbatim when the query matches table headers. Text-formatted "tables" (using `|` characters) are not extracted. Real table markup is the cheapest format change with the highest citation lift.

rule.statement: Step-by-step content MUST use numbered lists with the first 2-4 words of each item in bold AS the step name. Example: "**1. Choose your provider.** Cloudflare, AWS, GCP differ on ...". Plain paragraphs labeled "step 1, step 2" do not satisfy this rule.

rationale.source: AIO step-extraction patterns; SERP feature parser behavior

rule.rationale: AIO's step extraction reads the bolded prefix as the step title. Without bolded prefixes, the parser falls back to the first sentence and frequently picks the wrong fragment. The format is mechanical; the lift is significant.

rule.statement: Each major section (H2) MUST end with a citation row referencing the primary sources backing the claims in that section. Inline links count if they appear at section end; a separate "Sources" line is preferred. Sections without citations are FORBIDDEN on AIO-target pieces.

rationale.source: E-E-A-T Trust criterion; AIO source-attribution survey

rule.rationale: AIO panels show source attribution that links to the cited page. Pieces with explicit citation rows are surfaced as sources at multiples of bare-prose pieces. Citation rows also reinforce the E-E-A-T trust signal beyond AIO.

rule.statement: Every AIO-target piece MUST contain a 30-50 word direct answer to the query, placed in the first 200 words and clearly demarcated (e.g. a TL;DR / Quick answer block). The answer is self-contained — readable without scrolling. The 40-word target is the AIO snippet length floor.
rule.rationale: AIO panels include a snippet of approximately 40 words. Pieces with a self-contained 40-word answer are extracted as-is; pieces without one are paraphrased by the AIO model (lower citation, lower control).

example.title: Definition + 40-word answer in first 200 words
example.code:
# What is Earned-Value Management for Software Projects?
**Earned-Value Management (EVM)** is a project-control technique that compares planned work, completed work, and incurred cost on a single time-series to produce schedule and cost performance indices.
**Quick answer:** EVM gives PMs three numbers — PV, EV, AC — that combine into SPI (schedule efficiency) and CPI (cost efficiency). Both should stay between 0.9 and 1.1 on a healthy project; outside that band signals slip or overrun.
example.note: r1 + r5 satisfied. Definition is bold-prefixed; quick answer is self-contained and within 40 words.

example.title: Generic essay opening, no definition, no quick answer
example.code:
# Earned-Value Management
In today's world of complex projects, knowing where you stand is critical. There are many techniques to measure progress, each with its own strengths and weaknesses. This article explores the long history of EVM, its theoretical foundations, and...
example.note: r1, r4, r5 violations. No definition, no quick answer, intro-tax language (see also `ai-content-quality-review` r4). AIO will skip this piece.
